data.title: Output routing: deliver extracted artifacts to destinations
data.description:
## Parent: #1

## Depends on: #3

## Summary

Route extracted artifacts from JSON output files to their final destinations. Extraction produces structured data; this issue is about delivering it somewhere useful.

## Destinations

| Extraction Type | Destination Options |
| -- | -- |
| Decisions | Append to `decisions.md`, create GitHub issue for review, or both |
| Learnings | Create file in `knowledge/learnings/`, or GitHub issue |
| Patterns | Create file in `knowledge/patterns/`, or GitHub issue |
| TODOs | Create GitHub issue with labels |

## Approach

### Option A: GitHub Issues (recommended default)

* Each extracted artifact → GitHub issue with appropriate labels
* User reviews, edits, closes (or converts to permanent knowledge)
* Low friction, built-in review workflow
* Labels: `decision-draft`, `learning-draft`, `pattern-draft`, `todo`

### Option B: Direct file creation

* Write directly to configured paths
* Useful for `decisions.md` append workflow
* Risk: noisy if extraction quality is low

### Option C: Both

* Create GitHub issue AND stage the file
* Issue links to the staged file for review

## Configuration

```toml
[routing]
decisions = "github-issue"  # or "file" or "both"
learnings = "github-issue"
patterns = "github-issue"
todos = "github-issue"

[routing.paths]
decisions = ".skogai/memory/decisions.md"
learnings = ".skogai/knowledge/learnings/"
patterns = ".skogai/knowledge/patterns/"
```

## CLI

```bash
claude-memory deliver              # process all undelivered extractions
claude-memory deliver --dry-run    # show what would be created
claude-memory deliver --type decisions --dest github-issue
```
